reasoning: While the Trump administration has aggressively purged climate data and fired significant portions of NOAA personnel (including the Climate.gov content team), completely taking down a major top-level government domain like climate.gov is bureaucratically and technically different from stripping its content. As of May 7, 2025, the website remains online. The forecast horizon is quite short (less than six weeks to July 1, 2025). The most likely scenario is continuous removal of content or lack of updates rather than an abrupt shutdown of the server infrastructure that would cause "Is It Down Right Now" to mark it as offline. Legal pushbacks (such as the successful farmers' lawsuit to restore some agricultural climate data) might also deter them from a complete dismantling of domains. Therefore, despite the heavy cuts to NOAA, it is more likely than not that the site itself will still technically respond to server pings by July 2025.
